reviews.review_text: Very old cabin and falling to bits in lots of places. Air con didn't work, every door sticky it off its rollers, draws about to fall apart. Very poor and VERY expensive. We had to pay 10% extra due to only staying one night and they have a two night minimum. We were offered extra blankets as it was a very cold night.
Great staff service at short notice. Very accommodating and understanding to our needs. Solid amenities, regularly cleaned, good sites, great location. Will absolutely stay again.
cabin was filthy television didn't work bathroom timber was all swollen from the shower, dirt under bed and in window runners, blinds moldy and curtain dirty would not recommend.
We were unlucky the weather was terrible but ketching up with family was great, next time we will pick a better cabins.
We had a great time. WE WILL BE BACK!
Great location and area, found the place on the pricier side for so many broken things, our camp site which was powered, you easily needed 25m lead to get power which was a joke,
Check in was fast and friendly, the place looked well maintained. Great amenities and the sites are great. In a great location and nicely priced. Will definitely be back.
Fantastic oark.  Great location. Close to awesome off lead dog friendly beach.  Walking distance to town.
It was a great relaxing spot. The park was relaxing and very close to the lake and the beach most enjoyable.

Overall, we felt the experience was not aligned with the price point, especially considering we paid over $1000 for the stay.||There were several concerns we’d like to highlight constructively:||At reception, the check-in process was not smooth, and unfortunately the staff member did not fully listen to our request regarding the two cabins.|While we appreciated being allowed to check in early, the standard 3pm check-in feels quite late, particularly when we observed cleaning staff on-site earlier in the day but not actively progressing rooms.||Cabin C10:||The bathroom door was not functional and could not be closed, which is not acceptable at this price level.|The overall cleanliness was below expectations — the floors, bathroom, and toilet all appeared unclean.|Bed linen was outdated and did not feel fresh.|The rubbish bin was broken.||Cabin GT1:||The space and location were great, and we appreciated the surroundings and atmosphere.|However, the fridge was not working properly and did not cool effectively. The freezer was also not functioning as expected.|The outdoor BBQ was adequate but did not reflect the premium pricing.|The lawn maintenance was carried out during our stay, which disrupted the experience — this should ideally be scheduled outside guest occupancy times.|The air conditioning and fans appear to need servicing or upgrading.||Cleanliness concerns (important):||The bed sheets in particular were unacceptable and must be addressed urgently from a hygiene and health perspective.|Sofas were heavily worn and stained, to the point we avoided using them comfortably.||On a positive note, the kids had a great time, and the location — especially the proximity to the beach and lake — is fantastic. Security also felt reassuring.||That said, for the price being charged, the standard of maintenance, cleanliness, and service needs significant improvement. We hope this feedback is taken seriously and addressed promptly
We stayed in a tiny house and it was great. It had everything you need. Short walk to the lake and beach. Slightly longer but very manageable walk to the main street shops

We’ve returned home from 8 nights in our caravan at Reflections Lennox Head, such an amazing, clean, well maintained park, staff couldn’t do enough for us, fantastic range of Easter Holiday activities. Perfect location, we didn’t need to use our vehicle other than a short drive to visit Ballina.
Congratulations once again to Dan and the team for making our holiday so enjoyable.
Scott & Dianne Hillier
Awesome
Great location beside beach and fresh water swimming lake.

The location was great. BUT. The cabin we stayed in the carpet was filthy it was covered in stains. All the cushions and lounge chairs were extremely dirty. The tv in the bedroom did not work. The main door the runners were worn out and I could barely move it and the windows looked like they haven’t been cleaned in months.

Just one the best caravan parks you can find... Really well kept, friendly staff and the location is just amazing.  Right next to Lake Ainsworth and the surf beach.. one of the few beaches in Ballina Shire that is off leash for our beloved furry family.

You can't go wrong... Come and stay... You'll be back again and again. Like us 😁

Lovely spot, and great location.
Very disappointed that having paid $100 plus per night for a powered camp site that with the rain we had this past weekend that all of our groups 5 site's flooded with 1 site experiencing 2 inches of water through the tent site!
It really doesn't take much to build up the camping sites with a bit of gravel and have proper drainage in place.
The Men's facilities main door was also ripped off it's hinges/missing, and 2 of the shower doors had no latches.
Great service and amenities. Had a lovely camping trip here. Will be back.

Reflections at Lennox Head is a family favourite. We spent 10 years holidaying, every September,  with our Parish group. So many wonderful memories. Going back in March with hubby and our dog Jack for a few days in paradise. Can't wait! Great service, wonderful amenities, close to everything and in an incredible location with the lake and beach on your doorstep. Whether camping, clamping or in between, Reflections at Lennox Head is perfect for your next getaway!
Great place to stay, close to all amenities, restaurants, pubs, beach and lake.  Everything you'll need is within walking distance.

Stayed a week.  Check in staff were both lovely.  Price wasn't great for a caravan site but it wasn't outrageous either.
Next time I would not stay at the site number we stayed at as there are better sites but it does get busy on weekends.
Amenities in block 4 were pretty clean apart from cobwebs in female showers (water pressure in female showers wasn't great buty husband said one in his had great pressure and one didn't) .  Little off the leash dog area inside the caravan park which is hot in the sun as no shade but fun for dogs early morning or before dusk.
Short walk to the off the leash dog beach which our dogs love. No cars in the beach (unlike Evans Head) so it's great for walking and swimming with dogs.  1.4 km walk to town for mini mart & bakery.
No dogs allowed at Lake Ainsworth but that's fair enough. Great little town for a week away but not a long drive from Brisbane.

Great location and amenities, the staff were accomodating and friendly. Love that there is a family room.
If you’re in a tent I’d avoid the “powered site no caravans” on a Friday night. Those sites are on the main thoroughfare, so cars were driving by to set up at night. The noise and headlights woke the kids (and us) each time.
Otherwise a great stay.
Staff in reception don’t understand how important customer service is. Safari tent also had animal crap under the couch.
Great location, spacious and clean and tidy.|Staff were friendly and helpful.

Abfab , check in staff lovely .cleaners moved buggies so it was easier to park caravan, we'd forgotten how beautiful lennox was .fantastic mini holiday .loved everything about the park .highly recommend

Although clean and safe facilities, very confusing check-in process after hours. You need codes to get into the property (which didn’t work for me) and to access the bathroom (easy to miss in instructions when you’ve been on the road all day). Lots are also very close to one another, so not much privacy.

Do not camp here. Small sites. They make it look like on the website it's surrounded by trees. Not the case. It's surrounded by high walls and housing. It's like camping in someone's back yard. Horrible. Very dishonest.
We stay here every year, it is awesome

Large, tidy, well maintained, camp-site. The amebities block is very clean. Short stroll to the beach. Both staff that i spoke to at the desk were polite and friendly. So were the cleaners and mowers (who were super sweet to my machinery obsessed toddler). Huge thanks to Elysia (?) for helping us to juggle and amend a 3-site booking during peak holiday time. See you next year!
Amazing location, right next to the lake and beach. Lots of activities for kids to participate in during school holidays.

The location is great, however unfortunately I wouldn’t return.

We experienced several issues during our stay. The manager was very rude and we were not allocated the site we had booked. We paid for a powered site, yet there was no water available. On top of that, we were overcharged due to staff not understanding or correctly applying their own policies.

The amenities were consistently not clean during our stay, which was disappointing for the price we paid.
Expensive, but if you can afford it... Sure. Friendly staff.
Surrounded by suburbia. It's more like camping in someone's backyard. Website is very misleading.
Situated in such a great spot close to the town, beach and Lake Ainsworth.
Great location & very friendly

The glamping tents are just an expensive overpriced tent. It’s very hot inside during the day. Not insulated enough for the air conditioning to cool anything down. It’s not as self contained as it states. Bring your own utensils and cooking equipment.  We were provided one spatula, no other equipment. |And it was very dusty. The wall fan was coated in thick dust, under the beds were covered in thick dust. And the mattress was so hard, we purchased a mattress topper for the remainder of our stay. |Honestly very overpriced and not good value. |It’s handy having an ensuite, but a hotel or Airbnb would be much better value. |The location is great, it’s a shame the glamping tents are not better presented.
Very deceptive. Surrounded by housing and tall fences. Small sites. Gazebo walls inches from each other. Nearly no trees. Definitely stay away.

Reflections Lennox Head Holiday Park is absurdly well placed.
It’s right on Lake Ainsworth, which means you wake up already winning. Tea-tree water, paperbark trees, light doing its gentle magical thing… honestly, it feels like someone put a holiday park inside a postcard. There are lots of people here—families, couples, floaty lake-dwellers—and yes, it costs a bit. But standing there looking at the lake, you find yourself nodding along like, Yes, this does seem like prime real estate, carry on.
It’s busy without being stressful, social without being loud, and somehow still feels a bit like a secret even though everyone knows about it.
You swim, you wander back to your site, you forget what day it is.
Five stars. For location, charm, and for the rare luxury of being able to roll out of bed and into a dreamlike lake before your brain has time to object.
The place is magnificent, the water at the lake is wonderful! The beach is beautiful

Hyvät puolet:
- Aivan upea ranta
- Viihtyisä ympäristö jossa oli monta eri hyvää ruokapaikkaa
(Suosittelen aamupalaa ”Papaya” ravintolassa)

Huonot puolet:
- Vessat hieman epäsiistit
Very well maintained park in wonderful location near lake and beach.  Fantastic pet friendly environment.  Great staff. Feels very safe.

Wir hatten den Campingplatz mit einer Powered Site auf Gras für 2 Nächte gebucht. Der Check in hat super funktioniert, obwohl wir erst nach Schliessung der Rezeption ankamen. Leider ist der Campingplatz sehr in die Jahre gekommen. Auch die Sauberkeit lässt zu wünschen übrig.
Wir hatten auf unserem Platz mehr Sand als Gras und die Abwasserleitung war nicht zu gebrauchen, da alles voller Blätter war.
Es hat vor allem Hundebesitzer auf diesem Campingplatz und wir hatten auf unserer Site Hundehaarebüschel im Gras, da unsere Nachbarin ihren Hund gebürstet hat.
Den Grill konnten wir nicht nutzen, da er sehr dreckig und noch mit Wurstresten drauf war. Wir sind nach eine Nacht bereits weiter gefahren.

Stayed on a powered site for a few days.
Good place to use as a base and check out other coastal towns.
Close to Byron Bay and Ballina.
Its next to a lake and across the road from surf beach.
A short drive to a new Woolworths.
Restaurants and coffee shops a walk or short drive along beach road.
Staff were very nice with clean amenities.
Dog friendly.
Location is perfect. Busy park with much traffic coming and going. Speed signs needed to slow vehicles. Friendly helpful staff and amenities clean

We camped for a few nights, and really enjoyed our stay. The Tea Trea lake is at your doorstep and the ocean 20 metres further away.
A less than 10 minutes walk to the shopping centre, cafės and specialty shops.
It's an even lesser walk to the local club for a meal and a cold ale.

Beautiful site with range of Australian Native Plants and walking distance to the lake and beach. The site and amenities were clean. The ladies at the reception and the staff member at the kitchen we briefly met (sorry don’t know their names) were friendly and professional. Great experience overall. Thanks for your hospitality.

Good clean amenities, with regular cleaning.
Unfortunately no cash accepted at the Office which is a problem. Staff were friendly, and a top location.
Awesome park in brilliant location and SUPER dog friendly. Clean amenities and friendly, helpful staff. Highly recommend 🌟🌟🌟🌟🌟
Beautiful place. We have a very nice cabin
Place is huge

This was one of my favourite campsites.the showers and toilets were amazing . This campground was close to all sorts of things:town was a walking distance away, it was only half an hour away from byron bay were we had the best surfing lesson, the  sunrise from the beach was a fabulous , eye catching view .

Lennox Head is always a fab campground to visit. The park is maintained well and the staff are always so friendly and efficient, nothing is ever too much trouble. |The location is amazing , so close to the beach, lake and the town

Could not complain about the cleanliness of our cabin and it was a good spot, however, it was extremely tired and thought it a bit expensive for the state it was in.
Bedside lights didn't work. Mattress on top bunk very sunken in the middle.
One of the best parks in NSW. Clean, well maintained and excellent staff. We’ll definitely come back again.
Great park with modern facilities. Dog friendly! Right next to the Tea Tree Lake and dog beach. Easy walk into the main part of Lennox Heads.

What a delightful place - close to the surf beach, and adjacent to Lake Ainsworth. I booked for five days then added a couple more. It's an easy drive to Ballina for shopping and Byron Bay is about the same distance north

Stayed in a delux cabin for 7 nights and had to borrow an inflatable mattress as all 3 beds were as bad as each other (Queen,
2 bunks)
Beds felt like they were very old and worn out.
Shame as the location to the beach is good

We stayed for one night and yes, the beach and lake are nearby and within walking distance of the campsite, but the amenity block was very average and the supplied boom code didn't work for us and so had to wait for someone to let us out.||So, Ok for one night as a stop on a longer journey.
About the visit the beautiful location.

We had a great stay from start to finish.
The park and amenities were clean and tidy and all in working order.
The office team sorted out a powered site for us after the Solar on our van played up and our unpowered site was not going to work.
I thought that the texts about tourism spots and activities was a great idea.
They also texted us a storm warning which was very helpful.
The location was awesome, close to the beach and Lennox coffee shops and restaurants.
We will stay again and have no problem recommending Reflections Lennox Head
Great park, would be great if there was somewhere to clean the fish caught at the beach.

Love Lennox Head, but disgusted with the standard of our cabiin in Reflections. Sandy floor, kitchen and bunks together. Couldn’t Lock Screen door, even the maintenance man couldn’t fix it. Sleeps 4, only 2 chairs on patio and a broken table you couldn’t lean on.
The camp kitchen was good, paper towels would’ve been handy. Needless to say won’t be staying there again or recommending it.

If you have your own campervan than you be comfy there, but if your hire a 'standard' cabin, be warned they are dirty and old and below standard quality for hotel price they charge per night. Nearly tripped o broken 5c times

They say you get what you paid for ... well at $ 249 a night for standard cabin price I expected a high quality clean cabin and not an old dirty run down cabin. I could have booked a 4x star hotel for this price!!!
Decent sized sites. Great location. Walking distance to everything.

Great park, very friendly, pet friendly and right on Lake Ainsworth and 7 mile beach. Dogs off the leash. Well done our first time here and we will definitely be back. Plus great fishing , caught 12 fish in 2 hours on the lake

We had such a fantastic stay at Lennox Head Caravan Park! From the moment we arrived, the customer service was warm, friendly, and welcoming. The facilities were spotless and well maintained, making everything so comfortable and stress-free. We were especially happy that the park is pet friendly. Our dogs had the best time and loved that the off-leash dog park was just a short stroll away.
A special mention has to go to the dump point. It was honestly the cleanest we’ve ever used, which made the whole experience so much easier. This park truly goes above and beyond to keep everything in top condition. We’ll definitely be back and highly recommend it to anyone looking for a clean, dog-friendly, and well-run caravan park in a beautiful location!
Wunderschöner Campingplatz. Alles Tipp top in Schuss, nette Leute, tolle Lage. Nähe Byron Bay. Wir haben die Lage sehr genossen.

Lovely spot. Looking forward to spending some time here! The staff are very friendly and helpful. The amenities and grounds are very well maintained. Lots of open spaces, so you don't feel crowded in.
Nice walk to the beach or town.
Just a beautiful spot!

It's a shame about the constant barking of dogs as people move about in the caravan park.

This is our 3rd time staying here. Great location. A bit disappointed by the cabin. TV very fuzzy, and couldn't get the bedroom one working. Shower pressure is barley there and after we loaded the dishwasher, we then found out the section where you put the tablet is broken.  Cabin next door air con unit is so loud you can hear it during the night. Don't know how they slept through it.
Love that we can bring our fur baby, but a shame its only in off season???
Very well kept communal amenities. Love the closeness of the beach
Lovely caravan park, great location, super friendly staff. Have upgraded driveway asphalt ,well done.

We have been staying at this holiday park for many years. Great location & friendly staff. It would be nice to see the parks amenities have some attention/renovations but otherwise, it’s neat and tidy

We just stayed at this park for two nights  with our toddler and baby. Meeting up with friends staying here as well.
Unfortunately as we were checking out we had terrible customer service from the lady in the office.
We had forgotten our key code for the boom gate and had asked for a code to get out.
This was a huge inconvenience apparently to the lady.
As we wanted to go down to camp kitchen to meet our friends first for a big breakfast together. She suggested we park outside the park and walk there.
We have a newborn baby and toddler and heaps of bags. Not an easy task.
She was very put out by this and really not friendly at all.
My partner reminded her we had just stayed two nights and spent $500 with them.

It does not make you want to stay again.
My toddler also burnt his finger on a light bulbs that sits next to the bed with no protection around it. Something we weren't going to bother mentioning.

Other than that we had a good stay, nice park and kitchen facilities were great.

Definitely need to improve customer service. She may need to be sent on some course to improve her communication skills.

The lady at reception was very friendly and efficient , certainly a great asset to this business as first impressions are very important I was in cabin number 8 , the key would not open the screen door , the lady at reception apologised and arranged  2 staff to open it with a  master key .I did not lock the screen door once I gained access .The television remote would not activate the Television . Maybe flat batteries but very disappointing .
Great park. Will stay longer next time.
Lovely place, highly recommended

The location is great, right beside the tea tree lake and the surf beach. Facilities are good, would be great to have access to a children’s toilet in the family bathroom. It does feel like the park is aimed more at older travelers rather than families as there is no playground or pool on site.
With the price of a powered site for two nights was $130 you would think you could have access to a washing machine without paying additional. It cost me $14 to wash two loads and an extra $8 for the dryer and $2 for washing liquid. bit ridiculous.

Excellent park with good range of camping sites.
The young lad that checked me in was really nice, very impressed.
Very green and clean park.
I think 3 maybe 4 amenities blocks.
The two I went into were excellent.
Our caravan site felt not too cramped. As coastal parks can be brutal.
Close to a lovely lake.
Surf club across the road with a little cafe.
The surf was insane.
Short walk to two cafes.
Approximately 15 minutes walk to the village.
I highly recommend staying here.
Our visit was too short.

Located next to a huge lake and beach..
Non powered water craft, great for walking, cycling, sunrise/sunset photos are amazing at the lake..caravan park is great and very helpful staff..dogs aloud

Just spent 5 days here. Fabulous, very clean  and tidy. Next time we're going to get spot at other end closer to beach .
Our little dog gave it a big paw up as well.

Our glamping tent exceeded expectations, beautifully constructed and laid out, with everything we needed and more.  Communication by staff was excellent, great location, clean and very reasonably priced for the location.

Beautiful clean quiet caravan park close to the beach and the tea tree lake.

Ammenities are neat and tidy and staff are friendly.

Updated review 2025. This is still a great location but more touristy nowadays and their higher prices reflect that. Be cautious of their cancellation policy it does not promote customer loyalty, particularly during peak season, when they are not at a vacancy disadvantage. We will reflect on past experiences before booking our next trip here.

Wonderful location right next to lake Ainsworth and the beach! The basic cabin was dated/simple but nice and the shared toilet/shower facilities were very well kept.
However some room for improvement - the aircon in the basic cabin (we were in C1) was riddled with mould inside the duct and smelled strongly of mildew so we couldn’t use it at all.
Also the Wifi is nonexistent which means you get barely any reception. This is fine if you are trying to have a relaxing holiday away from your phone, but I imagine it would be difficult if you needed access to the internet for something.

Overall pleasant stay and easy check in/out at a beautiful location. Needs some fine tuning and definite improvement for the price point.
Very overpriced for a caravan park/ cabins. Won't return at these prices. Could have stayed at a 5 star resort for same price.

They charged me after checking out for supposedly leaving a wet (pee) mattress on the top bed bunk… errr what?! No one slept on that bed, it was untouched. I talked to manager to explain it was impossible since the only thing that touch that mattress was my suitcase. They didn’t refund the charge.
Manager is crazy, totally lost the plot. Don’t even attempt to come to reason with this man.
Perfect place for a family holiday
Congested site. Location is amazing and convinient for lake and beach access. No play area or pool for kids in park. Old but reasonably clean amenities.
Small cabin on the river was perfect for us and our 6mth old
Cannot do a report on this as we only went there for a look see. Looks like a lay back tourist area with a caravan park and two shops.
Excellent place for the family and friends with pets
Fantastic caravan park near in an awesome seaside town
It's one of our favourite caravan parks. So close to the dog friendly beach and walking distance to the main street..

Had the most relaxing magical weekend camping at Lennox Reflections Park, super friendly helpful staff, clean amenities, large campsites and beautifully looked after grounds. The camp kitchen was really beautiful too! Highly recommend. Central location to everything in Lennox, we took our bikes and rode around everywhere. Will definitely be back!

I camped here and the sites were large with easy access to power/water. The amenities were functional and in good condition but did not reflect the 'new' condition depicted on the website. The customer service was friendly, but I did have issues contacting anyone for help.

The location is superb and I would definitely recommend this for a camping holiday

We have just finished a road tripping the entire east coast of Australia and we fell in love with Lennox Head.
We only stayed at reflections lennox head for 1 night which turned out, it was no where near enough time. The gorgeous lake was a highlight of our trip. Next time we do the trip, we will stay here again and for longer.
Nice water sports
Good park, great service and perfect location, just not on busy weekends
So, so good. I need to return. So close to everything, made for more relaxation time

Lovely location, pleasant & quiet.
Good choice.
Always nice to stay here close to beach and lake staff friendly rear amenities block Gould do with an upgrade compared with the others
Excellent caravan park , right on beach, dog friendly
Infraestrutura e localização excelentes.

The location is stunning, across from the beach and next to the tea tree lake. However, the exhaust fan in the bathroom was hanging from the ceiling and looked like someone tried to hide a stash in it and a mattress 2cm off the floor on a wooden plank does NOT constitute a "2nd bunk". To top it off, in the pouring rain their boom gate wouldn't work with the code not once but twice and we had to wait for somebody entering fir us to to leave the premises almost being collected by the boom on our way out. If there was any other caravan/camping sites in Lennox we would stay there next time. Alas, Reflections has the monopoly and at $180/night for their cabins,  I highly recommend you spend your hard earned $ somewhere else. :-(

We had an overnight stay to see how we went with our caravan and we loved it here! Easy to access, easy to get around inside the park, no difficulty parking our caravan. The staff were delightful and welcoming. The location is perfect and there’s easy access to all facilities in the park and it’s close to the main part of town. It’s also dog friendly so we were able to bring our senior dog who requires medication twice a day.
Beautiful spot, close to restaurants and hotels. Dog friendly
Great caravan and camping park lots of choice of sites. Close to beach . Close to coffee shop. Close to dog friendly beach run. Plenty of fish.

We just stayed a week at this beautiful, well maintained holiday park.
Situated right by the beach.
Easy access to beaches, township and great walking and mountain bike riding tracks along the coast to Ballina.
The park has plenty of trees and real grass!
The roads inside the park are quite pot holed and the speed bumps are very, very effective!
Would stay here again, beautiful township and lovely places to eat out.
The lake/inlet is very spectacular and everyday produces a different view on the lake/inlet.

We recently stayed at Reflections Lennox Head and, while there were some positives, there are significant issues that need to be addressed urgently. The most glaring problem is the condition of the roads and drainage system. The roads within the park are in terrible shape, full of potholes and uneven surfaces, making it difficult to drive or walk safely. Additionally, after a bout of rain, the drainage system proved to be wholly inadequate, leading to large pools of standing water that made several areas of the park nearly impassable.

On a positive note, the park offers a great location and the staff were friendly and accommodating. The amenities, such as the bathrooms and recreational facilities, were clean and well-maintained. However, the overall experience was significantly marred by the poor infrastructure.

Hopefully, management will take these concerns seriously and invest in the necessary repairs to improve the experience for future guests.
Clean and well maintained spacious park, with friendly staff and the most fabulous location!

The welcome by Alicia was exceptional, a rare and refreshing touch in the camping world. Rest assured, the campsite is spotless with super clean blocks and a great team keeping everything in top shape. Road access is smooth with no potholes like in older reviews.

I stayed there 6 years ago, and it was okay, but now it’s fantastic. Fully dog-friendly, not just a designated area anymore, so everyone’s on the same page. Non-dog lovers can simply book one of the other 80% of Australian campsites that aren't dog-friendly. I love the quick beach access, the pleasant just 20-minute drive from Byron Bay and the dog beach next to the campsite.

I stayed on unpowered sites near the lake (along the fence). They have a great view and dedicated access to the lake/beach through three gates. There might be a bit of traffic at night around the lake, but nothing major. Note that sites T87 to T89 are not fully flat. This isn't an issue if you have a caravan or van, but could be one if you have a big tent or two tents. You'll have to set up your tent at the front of the site where your car is parked, near the road access. It’s a bit of a pity since the sites are generous in size, but the back is unusable. Check with reception before arriving.

All in all, I highly recommend the camping if you have dogs or just want a nice spot in the Byron Bay area. Lennox is improving year after year with more shops and restaurants, which is great for splitting your time between the two towns. I also really appreciated the calmness of the park during this winter period—very relaxing.

Great location. Great facilities. Rooms in cabins are drab and need attention. Road also full of potholes. No facilities for coal bbq. Lennox a lovely town. Beautiful beaches. 4/4 pass for the beach right there too.
We stayed overnight in our van. Across the road from the beach. Good facilities. Dog yard in grounds.
Friendly staff, clean amenities, and a nice quiet place to stay.

On a positive side the park is centrally located staff are very courteous and the park very clean with up-to-date amenities
The sites are mainly level many have concrete slabs with well maintained grass elsewhere
On a negative side Cash is not accepted in the office and kiosk
The roadways need attention as there are numerous deep potholes throughout the park
Friendly staff, great location, clean facilities, everything you need for a great holiday

Stayed at a cabin and it was spectacularly good.  Comfortable all the amenities with a short walk to the beach or to the lake.  Friendly accommodating staff to assist with booking and knowledge of the area.

We have just finished our 2 week stay with Reflections Lennox Heads. Our stay in the their dog friendly cabins was amazing. All the staff are  friendly and do an amazing job, we were well looked after the whole 2 weeks. Lennox Heads is one of our favourite places on the NSW North Coast 💖
Great place.Love it with my family and friends

We've stayed here a couple of times and really love the location.
The amenities are good and the grounds are well kept.
Biggest drawcard is probably the beautiful beach across the road.

Loved the glamping tent we stayed in. It was clean, cleverly designed, and offered all we needed for a few days in Lennox. The tent had a queen bed, 2 single beds, a bathroom and kitchenette. The patio also had a bbq and outdoor bath (which had no water supply so unsure if they were still installing it). Service from reception was great too.
Noisy people until 2 am, after hours number did not pick up. Would not recommend.
Beautiful park, very friendly staff, fantastic amenities.
Good location near the lake and a short walk to the beach
Great location but cabin 15 was filthy .  Would not recommend

Driveways need serious attention, paths and access paths to lake and generally are not wheel chair friendly, etc are totally unusable with my wheel chair.
The door on the easy access bathroom is to heavy to push open from a wheel chair,
Staff are great, need a special area where you put vans with pets together.
I am not again.st animals but barking dogs are annoying and I didn't come he for that ,I am and was here for rehabilition purposes

Good service, great location. The toilets showers and site was filthy. The roads and facilities are in urgent need of repairs and maintenance.
Very relaxing atmosphere.
Our stop afterwards was at Iluka Bimbimbi Riverside cpark, which was older but immaculately kept.
Spent two nights here on our way down south. Friendly staff and nice big sites. One plus was there was one big slab for the van and the awing together.  Walking distance to town centre and the beach.

I am absolutely appalled, disgusted and disappointed on the customer service I received. I contacted reception regarding a death in our family that occurred and explained we would like to transfer our stay to another time of year. The funeral had been planned (out of my control) right in the middle of what was to be our stay, and was held in Sydney. Rendering my family to be in a point to chose to go on holiday or support our family and grieve.

The lady I spoke to and explained the circumstances was rude and I am absolutely disgusted in her lack of compassion and sympathy. I was not expecting a refund at all, and hopeful to transfer our stay to another time of year, as I have knowledge that other holiday camp grounds offer this option.

I understand there are policies, however given the time of year, and having knowledge of many parks we have travelled to, I am aware that it is quite easy to fill a spot that is vacant during this busy holiday period.

We travel a lot, as well as my family and many friends. My father, who was the other party unable to make their visit is a tour guide for Tagalong Tours, in Queensland. He has assured me that on his tours he will be sure to advise his guests as well as many camper friends (whom travel quite frequently up and down between Qld and SA) of how we were disregarded and just a number and pay check to your holiday park. You have not only lost on us for future stays but on a lot of potential people that I’m sure will further express to their friends and family.

During a very sad time, where I was hopeful someone would be understanding, I was hurt and disappointed. Absolutely disgusting.
Love this place. Location location location. Bonus pet friendly.

Great stay! Staff were friendly, efficient and helpful - we booked into an unpowered site last minute with no problems. Clean and safe. Great location. Straight across from the beach and the lake which is a lovely swim! Reasonably priced in off periods, however, more expensive in peak periods (as per the standard).
Would recommend. Thank you!! 🤩

Huge potholes  in the access roads ,
Rubbish bins are to far apart and not managed or emptied in a timely manner ,were left over flowing , amenities average at best ,not kept clean allowed to get dirty,
Great spot...fresh t tree water and surf
Tea tree water in Lake Ainsworth amazing

Stayed at a powered site. Lovely location. Quiet. Lots of trees. The road in the park is full of speed bumps and potholes which is not a great combo.

Love the ti tree lake Ainsworth.
The caravan park is dog friendly. No dogs at the lake or the flagged area of the beach. Dogs allowed just north of that.
Caravan Park, excellent place to vacation near the beach, restaurant and spectacular red water
An awesome place to stay. Close to the beach. Great for dogs. We don't have kids but the kids in the park were having a ball. Looking forward to our next stay.
Great location between Lake Ainsworth and the surf beach. Chilled atmosphere. No frills and that's the way we like it.
The roads in park are not flash, lots of stuff need repair or update, won't be the staff, be reflections,  they like taking money & not keeping their parks up to scratch.
Even though the weather wasn't the best we still had a great time

Lennox Heads is one of our favourite holiday destinations on the east coast.
The booking and checkin at Reflections was friendly but could have been more helpful when we asked a couple of times to extend our stay. The park is in a prime location, at the beach (dog friendly ) walking distance to shops, cafes and restaurants. FYI (Be aware the road in has a lot of large pot- holes that are trip hazards). We love it here and will be back
Clean, tidy cabin. But not what was advertised.  Very expensive just for a basic cabin. Will not return.
I wasn't expecting the tent to be so very good. Air con,spacious,own bathroom,lounge, bbq,all modern conveniences.

Hi everyone
We are staying on site  PT 36. Directly across from our site is a man who lives as a permanent resident .He uses his blower to blow leaves and dust away  from his " shack" over the gravel road . The issue is that it generates a dust storm and the dust covers the vans/ tents  completely. We were outside our van cooking food when he blew the huge plumes of dust straight onto us. Our first reaction was to take the blower off him  however when we leave for daytrips our possessions are vulnerable. We now need to clean inside van , bbq , all outside gear because of this fool. We don't want other campers surrounding his shack to experience what we just did. Happy days! 😁

The location of this holiday park was the only good thing. Unfortunately, we weren’t impressed with room C13.
1. It was very dirty, i.e. I swept the floor shortly after arriving and found a lot of hair, dust and sand.
2. The screen door at the entry didn’t latch and had the lock installed upside down, not much security!
3. The window on right hand side when stepping into the room didn’t shut properly and had to be forced closed and locked to make it secure.
4. The light fittings in the entire room were dirty and the ones above the double bed were completely rusted, we were fearing our kids might accidentally break them and get electrocuted.
5. The aircon wasn’t properly working, the swivel was stuck open and luckily we didn’t have to use it!
6. The kitchen was dirty, one of the kitchen doors was missing and we chose not to use any of the utensils provided, as we were worried we’d get seriously sick from the grub.
7. The sliding door between the 2 rooms was outright dangerous and could’ve fallen off and hurt someone seriously at any moment.
8. The bunk beds should be vacuumed underneath the mattresses and checked for mould. I tucked in the blanket in the top bunk first and the below bunk had a lot of dust, sand and other particles all over it afterwards! Also, as soon as I lifted up the mattresses it smelled very mouldy and there were traces of black mould on the sheets.
9. The hot water in the shower wasn’t running properly by itself, although when turning on the cold tap as well it mixed well enough to still be warm at least.
10. The toilet definitely needs a new seat.

And to top it off, there was a message on the wall that there would be charges if “excessive cleaning” was necessary on departure! Like seriously? How about getting the cabin cleaned up in the first place!!

This cabin was so outrageously disgusting and bad, it was laughable. The only regret we have is not taking photos! We’ve never stayed in a disgusting place like this before and hopefully never will again!

We expected this cabin to be small and age is not an issue for us. However, the entire cabin needs a proper deep cleanse and it needs to be checked for mould. The smell is still bothering me! I don’t even want to think about the mites and things you can’t see but are definitely present in that cabin!

You should be ashamed to advertise this as a 3-Star accommodation.

Staff at reception were incompetent overcharging for our stay. Then had difficulty refunding the $291 overcharge. Blamed the computer.
Amenities were not clean and had a very bad smell. Mouse droppings on the sinks.
Clump of hair in same place for 10 days.
In the kitchen there were only 2 BBQs for the whole park and one was not working long term.
Drainage an issue after heavy rain. Roads had huge potholes.
Location is fantastic just a shame about everything else.
Bad water drip in downpipe and pillows too big and hard otherwise great

Staying in our van. Fabulous position close to beach and a coffee at the surf club. William street cafe is amazing. The staff here are so accomodating and really helpful. We added an extra night cos we love it here so much. Great for our doggie too.
Beautiful location near lake and surf.  The staff were fantastic.  I had a view of the lake from my unpowered site.
Lakeside & beach available 😎 spoilt for choice ,beautiful location, enjoy your stay 😊
Beautiful part of the world, close to the beach. Tidy well kept park. Would definitely stay again.

Beautiful holiday park..
Kids activities - pedal carts, stand up paddleboards, kayaks, to hire $10 / h

Showers and bathroom facilities are ok, (probably need a cleaner on in the evening when bathroom usage is peak).

Movie area set up for families to watch films .. daily updates of activities within the park.

Easy to get around, well equipped camp kitchen. 🏕 we loved our stay, and will be back!

The location was great. Excellent customer service and support throughout, just some points to be improved: electronic gate system for cars needs a service or upgrade, often would not open with correct code or freeze, toilet amenities, just need some repair ex shower heads broken and tiles and grout/ sealant.  The main road through the park is riddled with pot holes and would really help if this was fixed for cars and pedestrians. Overall it’s just maintenance and repairs that affected a great place to stay.
As empty as I've seen this park. Probably the cost of the sites.

Spotless facilities. Wide  level spots. Close to everything. Brooke and Dan are very professional  and the park is cleaned , patrolled and organised. Pets are welcome and dog beach close by. Across the road from beach and Lake Ainswoth. Highly  recommend.

They cater for the everyday traveller and families travelling in campers tents buses and caravans with cabins we were in our caravan youll enjoy the peaceful tranquillity of lake Ainsworth 👍❤😊 The managers and all staff are great they will stop for a chat and make sure your happy with your stay very pleased to encourage anyone to stay and unwind in a tranquil setting so close to the beach and the infamous Lake Ainsworth.
The beach is just across the road and if you have dogs the park now has a dog run for you to take your pets to and also theres a spot to take your dogs on the beach as sign posts are everywhere. We enjoyed our walks along the beach and up town plus on the weekends the surfers are usually out also through the week youll spot them too and if your in the right season for the whales you can see them from the shore at the lennox head cafe life saver location  😀 🐋🚶‍♀️🚶‍♂️☀️🏄‍♀️🏄‍♂️

The location of this park is amazing right opposite an off leash beach. I got a drive-through site the downside is it would be great if they had more of them but the staff at reception especially the Saturday morning girl was wonderful, spoke to you by using your name and nothing was too much trouble. The Dogwash was great the pressure wasn’t but I love that reflections has a Dogwash. Definitely would be back to explore more not just an overnight stay
Great van Park. Close to beach, lake and dog friendly. Thoroughly recommend.

The only accommodation we could get was a budget pet friendly cabin which was disgusting mould on the ceiling and blinds dog hair on the blankets kitchen cupboard were rotting away nothing in the cabin was clean I am sorry to say it's the worst cabin I have ever stayed in.
Big park good large van sites. Pleasant staff. Good location near beach.
I loved staying here.  I was in a cabin. It had everything I needed. An amazing location near to all the great things in Lennox.  I didn’t want to leave.
Top little van park, pretty friendly and walking distance to beach and restaurants plus the beautiful lake Ainsworth right next door, excellent!
Great park and close to parks Lake and surf
Quiet, peaceful,relatively cheap ( was cheaper online than in person), we didn't want to leave the town is just a small walk. Great vibes here will definitely be back. Clean amenities 😁
